title: Welder

rationales.taskAutomation: Field and structural welding demands manual dexterity and real-time visual judgment that current robotic and AI systems can't replicate outside fixed factory cells.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic welding is mature in high-volume manufacturing, but that tooling doesn't reach field and structural welders working one-off joints on site.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in automotive and heavy-manufacturing plants; construction and field welding remain almost entirely manual.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can track weld logs, certifications and inspection paperwork, but cannot run a bead or pass a destructive weld test.

tasksAtRisk: Weld procedure documentation and certification tracking
tasksGrowing: Structural and pressure-vessel welding requiring certified judgment
Reading and adapting to non-standard field joints and repairs
Cross-training on new alloys and welding processes as materials evolve
salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 51-4121, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Field welding stays low-risk because robotic welding, though mature, is confined to fixed factory cells and hasn't reached one-off structural and site work. The exposure that exists is administrative: certification tracking and inspection logs, not the weld itself.
